div.head: The Ambassador in the Soviet Union (Kennan) to the Secretary of State
div.p:
4222. The Embassy has received a note from Foreign OfficeFor text of Soviet note No. 74 of November 2, 1944, see Department of State Bulletin, July 22, 1945, p. 130. (regarding Department’s airmail instruction No. 279 of August 21)See footnote 34, p. 1094. stating in effect: (1)
The Soviet Government appreciates the efforts of the American Government directed towards extirpating illegal traffic in opium and towards production and use of opium only for medicinal and scientific purposes; (2)
The question of a postwar conference and of the terms which might be included in a convention for restricting the cultivation of poppies is being studied by the competent Soviet authorities at the present time; (3)
With reference to proposal that the Soviets issue a declaration concerning the restriction of opium production to the amount indispensable satisfying only medicinal and scientific needs, the existence of a state monopoly both in the field of production and distribution makes the issuance of such a declaration unnecessary.
